name: pymemcache (Python Memcached Client)

auth.notes: Memcached has no default auth; SASL optional

performance.notes: In-memory cache; sub-millisecond for local; 1-5ms network

agent_readiness.notes: Python Memcached client from Pinterest. from pymemcache.client.base import Client. client = Client(('localhost', 11211)). client.set('key', 'value', expire=300). client.get('key'). client.delete('key'). client.get_many(['k1', 'k2']). client.set_many({'k1': 'v1', 'k2': 'v2'}). client.add('key', 'value') → only if not exists. client.replace('key', 'value') → only if exists. client.incr('counter', 1). client.decr('counter', 1). client.cas('key', 'new_value', cas_token). client.gets('key') → (value, cas_token). PooledClient for connection pooling. HashClient for consistent hashing across servers: HashClient([('host1', 11211), ('host2', 11211)]). Serialization: JSON, pickle. Retry on connection errors. No persistence — purely in-memory cache.

gotchas: No persistence — data lost on restart
No data structures beyond string/bytes — unlike Redis
Key size limit: 250 bytes; value size limit: 1MB by default
No auth by default — firewall-based security required
No pub/sub, streams, or scripting — simple key-value cache only
best_when: Simple distributed caching where Redis features aren't needed
High-read workloads needing multi-server consistent hashing
Legacy PHP/Django/Rails apps already using Memcached
When Memcached's simplicity and lower memory overhead is preferred
avoid_when: When persistence is needed (use Redis or Valkey)
Data structures beyond strings (Redis has lists, sets, sorted sets, etc.)
summary: pymemcache is a Python Memcached client from Pinterest. Client(('host', 11211)); client.set('k', 'v', expire=300); client.get('k'); client.get_many(['k1','k2']). PooledClient for connection pooling. HashClient for consistent hashing across servers. CAS operations. Apache-2.0. No persistence, no data structures, 1MB value limit. Best for simple distributed caching. Use Redis/Valkey when persistence or data structures are needed.
